title: waitzia refracta
intro: Waitzia refracta, also known as the pygmy grevillea, is a small shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its yellow or green flowers and ability to grow in arid conditions. Traditionally used by the Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, Waitzia refracta has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce the risk of infection. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including relief from fever, arthritis, and digestive issues, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in the prevention of chronic diseases. Waitzia refracta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
